scope: Record the TileLang compact sparse-dKV single-write pad4 branch and why it should not be pursued.

correctness.read: The branch is numerically viable on the small correctness gate.

latency.read: singlew_pad4 regresses the target S4096 timing despite the same MFMA count and a solved final fold.

counter_comparison.read: The pad4 branch keeps the same BF16 MFMA work and wave count but adds about 14.5% more SQ instructions and more VMEM reads. Lower VGPR pressure does not offset the extra control/index/load work.

decision.next_work: Do not spend more time on single-write pad geometry. Use the e2e bridge audit to move toward a fuller TileLang sparse-MLA backward boundary.

commands: S128 correctness run on do-sonle-kernel in rocm/sgl-dev:rocm720-mi35x-8c3b5aa-20260521-DSv4-tilelang-fragconvert-v4full
S4096 timing10 run on do-sonle-kernel in rocm/sgl-dev:rocm720-mi35x-8c3b5aa-20260521-DSv4-tilelang-fragconvert-v4full
rocprofv3 primary SQ counter pass for singlew_pad4
